mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and liquidity conditions, which lifts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is already above neutral.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of instances after FEDFUNDS first crosses below its prior 3-month average, or if the effect disappears in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE reflects labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which compresses earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of unemployment-shock instances, or if the sign is neutral in any 2-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand recovery in consumption and housing, which supports cyclical earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint up-moves in RSAFS and HOUST are not followed by above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the same sign without interaction benefit.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and margin pressure, which reduces cyclical profit expectations and causes negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day returns remain positive after clear INDPRO trend breaks, or if the signal fails to outperform an unconditional baseline over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive and PAYEMS is also rising, the economy is expanding on both output and payroll fronts, which supports earnings breadth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and payroll gains do not improve 60-day SPX returns relative to when only one of the two improves, or if the interaction term is insignificant in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated CPIAUCSL inflation level combined with a FEDFUNDS rate that lags inflation tightens real financial conditions, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high inflation plus lagging policy rates do not correspond to below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction adds no predictive power over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE during a monetary easing cycle signals resilience in domestic demand, allowing rate cuts to re-rate equities upward and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines in the last 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if rate cuts accompanied by stable unemployment do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the sign flips negative in easing episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in RSAFS after prior strength marks a demand rollover that reduces sales momentum for large-cap firms, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Reject if repeated RSAFS declines do not precede below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the pattern fails in consecutive late-cycle samples over a 3-year rolling window.
